content:
We are not actively recruiting for any permanent positions, but are always accepting applications for enthusiastic part-time front desk positions.

 

Front Desk Position

Although there are not many shifts available at the moment, we’re always happy to hear from enthusiastic, customer-oriented people who are interested in working at the front desk. If the description below sounds appealing to you, then please drop us a line!

The Edmonton Squash Club is a Squash / Fitness Club located at 111 Ave and 153 St, a short drive from UofA, Grant MacEwan and NAIT.  The ESC caters to all levels of squash, within a relaxed, private, social atmosphere.

We are searching for independent, outgoing, individuals to staff the front desk from 4 PM to midnight weeknights and 10-6 PM weekends. This job would be perfect for an active University or College student looking for low-stress work after class, or aspiring personal trainers looking to network with potential clientele.

Duties include customer service, court bookings, selling memberships and merchandise, and light food / beverage service.

Skills that we are looking for:

  • Customer service and cash handling experience
  • Great conversational and interpersonal skills
  • Self-starter who doesn’t need much day-to-day supervision
  • A general interest in sports and fitness

It would also be an asset to:

  • Currently have, or be willing to work towards, First Aid, CPR Level C and AED certifications
  • Currently have, or be willing to work towards Pro-Serve Certification from the AGLC
  • Ability to use a computer for Point of Sales, reply to emails and other basic computer tasks
  • Familiar with Squash or interested in trying the game out

A typical work week will be approximately one or two 6-8 hour shifts,  sometimes we require extra staffing during tournament weekends. We generally host one tournament a month January – April.

Successful candidates will have full access to the squash club on days that they are working – great for a pre or post-shift workout.

Please send a short (no more than 2 paragraphs) statement of why you think you’d be a good fit, and a CV (max 1 page) covering things you would like us to know about you (jobs, hobbies, interests), to jobs@edmontonsquashclub.ca, along with 1 – 3 references from people you have worked with in the past (job, volunteer, group projects, sports etc…)
